channel.title: Origami on Obert Guo
channel.description: Recent content in Origami on Obert Guo

item.title: Paper Division
item.description: Introduction In origami, there may be times where a 13 x 13 grid or an 11 x 11 grid is needed. Such “indivisble” grids are hard to fold without measuring or cutting strips off, since realistically, the paper can only be accurately bisected. Method One way to fold an N x N grid is to use reference points. Let us plot a square on a Cartesian plane, with the bottom-left corner of the square placed about the origin.
